data.title: PRECIOUS METALS | Hong Kong Gold Exchange Announces Closing Prices
data.description: The Hong Kong Gold Exchange has released the closing prices for last Friday, the 13th. According to Ming Pao, the price for 99 gold was 46,425 HKD, while the kilogram gold bar was priced at 1,252.3 HKD.

data.title: Bithumb will list Lighter (LIT) and open trading pairs for the Korean Won.
data.description: Bithumb announced that it will list Lighter (LIT) on the Korean Won market. Trading is expected to begin at 7:00 PM on February 16, 2026.

data.title: Malaysia Urged to Intensify Anti-Corruption Efforts
data.description: Sultan Ibrahim Iskandar of Johor has called on Malaysia's government to enhance its efforts in combating corruption. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the Sultan's appeal for stronger measures to address graft within the country. Sultan Ibrahim emphasized the importance of transparency and accountability in governance, urging authorities to take decisive action against corrupt practices. His remarks come amid growing concerns over corruption's impact on Malaysia's economic and social development. The Sultan's call for action reflects a broader demand for integrity and ethical conduct in public service, aiming to restore public trust and ensure a fair and just society.

data.title: Mizuho Financial Group's Brokerage Unit Under Investigation
data.description: The Securities and Exchange Surveillance Commission is conducting an investigation into Mizuho Financial Group's brokerage division. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the scrutiny faced by the financial institution. The probe aims to ensure compliance with regulatory standards and assess the brokerage's operations. Mizuho Financial Group, a major player in the financial sector, is cooperating with the authorities to address any concerns raised during the investigation. The outcome of this inquiry could have significant implications for the brokerage's future operations and regulatory standing.

data.description:
The vacation rental market will surpass $120 billion by 2027. Building a marketplace to capture even a small slice of that used to mean hiring developers, spending six figures, and waiting half a year for a minimum viable product. That math has changed. AI-powered development tools now generate full-stack web applications from plain text descriptions
source: https://www.ethnews.com/how-to-build-an-airbnb-clone-with-ai-in-under-an-hour/

data.title: Inflation Rate Increases by 9.6% in January
data.description: Consumer prices experienced a significant rise of 9.6% compared to the previous year in January. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the ongoing inflationary pressures affecting the economy. This increase in consumer prices reflects the broader trend of rising costs across various sectors, impacting household budgets and purchasing power. Economists are closely monitoring these developments as they assess the potential implications for monetary policy and economic growth. The data underscores the challenges faced by policymakers in balancing inflation control with economic stability.

data.title: Emerging Market Currencies Strengthen as Offshore Yuan Hits Three-Year High
data.description: Emerging market currencies saw a slight increase, with the offshore yuan reaching its highest level in nearly three years. Bloomberg posted on X that this development highlights the growing strength of the yuan in the global market. The offshore yuan's performance is seen as a positive indicator for emerging market currencies, reflecting broader economic trends and investor confidence. This rise in the yuan's value comes amid ongoing economic adjustments and policy shifts in major economies, influencing currency markets worldwide.

data.title: OpenClaw Founder Joins OpenAI, Open Source Commitment Continues
data.description: Peter Steinberger, the founder of the recently popular artificial intelligence tool OpenClaw, announced on Monday, the 16th, that he is joining OpenAI. According to Ming Pao, OpenAI CEO Sam Altman confirmed the news on social media platform X, stating that OpenClaw will remain open source and accessible to the public, with OpenAI providing support to ensure it is not privatized.

data.title: Bitcoin Rebounds Amid Bearish Signals and Rising Selling Pressure
data.description: Bitcoin experienced a rebound of nearly 9% from February 12 to 15, leading to increased futures open interest and funding rates as traders anticipated further gains. According to NS3.AI, this upward movement occurred within a bearish flag pattern, accompanied by hidden bearish divergence. Additionally, there was a sharp 90% increase in unrealized profits, indicating potential selling pressure. Key support levels are identified near $66,270 and $58,880, with a break below these levels possibly resulting in a 14% price decline toward $58,000.

data.title: STOCKS | European Stock Futures Show Modest Gains
data.description: European stock futures experienced slight increases, with the Euro Stoxx 50 index futures rising by 0.13%. According to Jin10, Germany's DAX index futures saw a 0.24% uptick, while the UK's FTSE 100 index futures climbed by 0.18%. These movements reflect a cautious optimism in the European markets.

data.title: STOCKS | Nikkei Index Experiences Decline, Fails to Maintain 57,000 Level
data.description: The Nikkei Index faced fluctuations and ultimately declined, unable to sustain the 57,000-point level. According to RTHK, the index initially rose by nearly 280 points, reaching 57,219 points. However, it closed at 56,806 points, marking a decrease of 135 points or 0.24%. This marks the third consecutive day of decline for the index.

data.title: CZ: Lack of privacy is the last obstacle to crypto payments.
data.description: At the 2026 World Economic Forum, Binance founder CZ stated that despite the rapid development of L2 networks, virtually no one uses cryptocurrency for payments in daily transactions. He pointed out that the main obstacle is no longer the high fees and slow speeds of the past decade, but rather the complete lack of transaction privacy on public blockchains. For example, businesses cannot use public ledgers to pay suppliers without exposing their full balances, other suppliers, and precise cash flows to competitors. CZ's vision is an "invisible track," where blockchain will serve as the backend infrastructure for existing payment providers, making transactions faster and cheaper without the end user's awareness, and the industry should pragmatically integrate with traditional finance. Regarding global regulation, he proposed a "regulatory passport" model, where a license obtained in a reputable jurisdiction can be recognized in multiple regions. CZ also predicted that cryptocurrency will become the native currency of the emerging autonomous AI agent "machine economy."

data.title: Saudi Arabia Increases Crude Sales to China Following Price Cut
data.description: Saudi Arabia has increased its crude oil sales to China for March loading, following a significant price reduction. Bloomberg posted on X that the kingdom lowered the price of its primary oil grade for Asian buyers to the lowest point in over five years. This strategic move aims to boost demand from its top importer, China, amid fluctuating global oil markets. The price adjustment reflects Saudi Arabia's efforts to maintain its market share in Asia, a crucial region for its oil exports. The decision comes as part of broader strategies to navigate the challenges posed by global economic conditions and competitive pressures in the energy sector.

data.title: Philippine Fintech Firm Maya Considers $1 Billion US IPO
data.description: Philippine financial-technology company Maya is exploring the possibility of a US initial public offering (IPO) that could generate up to $1 billion. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting Maya's interest in joining other Southeast Asian firms in listing their shares on international exchanges. This move reflects a growing trend among companies in the region to seek capital from overseas markets, aiming to expand their global presence and access a broader investor base. Maya's potential IPO underscores the increasing appeal of the US market for Southeast Asian businesses looking to scale their operations and enhance their financial standing.

data.title: Analysis: Bitcoin has fallen 22.3% year-to-date, potentially marking its worst first quarter performance since 2018.
data.description: Data shows that Bitcoin has fallen 22.3% since the beginning of the year, potentially marking its worst first quarter performance since 2018, when it fell 49.7%. Bitcoin started the year trading at approximately $87,700 and has now fallen by about $20,000 to around $68,000. Furthermore, Bitcoin may be poised for its first consecutive month of declines in January and February. Analyst Daan Trades Crypto, writing on the X platform, stated that the first quarter is known for its volatility, and historically, events in the first quarter typically do not carry over into later periods. Nick Ruck, director at LVRG Research, stated that the continued decline in Bitcoin's price reflects a normal correction phase rather than a structural collapse in the asset's long-term trajectory. Historical patterns show that Bitcoin's resilience often leads to strong recoveryes in the following months. ETH has fallen 34.3% so far this quarter. Bitcoin fell 2.3% in the past 24 hours, trading at $68,670.

data.title: A whale deposited 732,000 VVV tokens into Coinbase and made a profit of $1.77 million.
data.description: According to Onchain Lens monitoring, in the past 24 hours, a whale (0x953...2b96) deposited 732,481 VVV tokens into Coinbase, worth $3.03 million, and made a profit of $1.77 million. This whale initially withdrew 763,725 VVV tokens from Coinbase, worth $1.56 million, then staked them for 6 months, ultimately depositing a total of 849,500 VVV tokens into Coinbase, worth $3.33 million.

data.title: DeFi Spot Trading Volume Triples Since Start of 2026
data.description:
Decentralized finance spot trading activity has surged sharply in the opening weeks of 2026, with total weekly volume rising nearly threefold compared to early January levels. The latest data shows aggregate DeFi spot volume climbing from roughly the low-$20 billion range in early January to nearly $80 billion by early February.
source: https://www.ethnews.com/defi-spot-trading-volume-triples-since-start-of-2026/
